api/v3/handlers/customers.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for core customer CRUD (list, create, get, delete, upsert) wired by api/v3/server; sub-packages (billing/, charges/, credits/, entitlementaccess/) own customer-scoped resource slices with distinct service dependencies and error-encoding needs. Primary constraint: namespace comes from the injected resolver, never from request params.

patterns.name: Public Handler interface + unexported handler struct + New()
patterns.description: handler.go declares a Handler interface listing every operation method (returning a typed handler alias) backed by an unexported handler struct holding resolveNamespace, service, and options. Sub-packages repeat this shape with their own service set.
patterns.example: type Handler interface { ListCustomers() ListCustomersHandler; CreateCustomer() CreateCustomerHandler; ... }; func New(resolveNamespace func(ctx context.Context) (string, error), service customer.Service, options ...httptransport.HandlerOption) Handler

patterns.name: Type-alias triplet per operation file
patterns.description: Each operation file (create.go, get.go, upsert.go, ...) defines <Op>Request/<Op>Response/<Op>Handler aliases before the method; path-param ops add <Op>Params and use HandlerWithArgs.
patterns.example: type ( CreateCustomerRequest = customer.CreateCustomerInput; CreateCustomerResponse = api.BillingCustomer; CreateCustomerHandler httptransport.Handler[CreateCustomerRequest, CreateCustomerResponse] )

patterns.name: Namespace resolved via injected resolver in decoder
patterns.description: Always call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) as the first step of the decoder closure; never read namespace from URL/query.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ListCustomersRequest{}, err }

patterns.name: apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder appended last
patterns.description: Every httptransport.AppendOptions ends with WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()); sub-package custom encoders are prepended before it.
patterns.example: httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("create-customer"), httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()))

patterns.name: Goverter for API-to-domain conversion
patterns.description: convert.go holds goverter:variables annotations; convert.gen.go (//go:build !goverter, DO NOT EDIT) is regenerated by make generate. Hand-written helpers (ConvertLabelsToMetadata, NamespaceFromContext) extend it.
patterns.example: // goverter:map . CustomerMutate\nFromAPICreateCustomerRequest func(namespace string, req api.CreateCustomerRequest) (customer.CreateCustomerInput, error)

patterns.name: Deleted-customer guard before mutation
patterns.description: upsert.go fetches the customer first, returns apierrors.NewGoneError when cus.IsDeleted(), and backfills the immutable Key before UpdateCustomer.
patterns.example: cus, err := h.service.GetCustomer(ctx, ...); if cus.IsDeleted() { return ..., apierrors.NewGoneError(ctx, errors.New("customer is deleted")) }; request.CustomerMutate.Key = cus.Key

key_file_guides.role: Declares the Handler interface and New() constructor listing every parent-level operation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing a method here causes a compile error when api/v3/server calls it.

key_file_guides.role: Goverter annotation source plus hand-written extend helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Omitting //go:generate header or goverter:variables block silently breaks regeneration.

key_file_guides.role: Goverter output (DO NOT EDIT) with init() setting conversion vars.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Hand-edits are overwritten by make generate.

key_file_guides.role: UpsertCustomer; pre-fetches customer to preserve immutable Key and guards IsDeleted().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Dropping the key-backfill wipes the customer key in the DB; dropping the IsDeleted check resurrects deleted customers.

key_file_guides.role: ListCustomers with page defaulting (1/20), sort via request.ParseSortBy, and per-field filters via filters.FromAPIFilterString/FromAPIFilterULID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Skipping page.Validate() accepts invalid page sizes; each filter[...] error must wrap apierrors.NewBadRequestError with the matching Field.

key_file_guides.role: Sub-package: customer billing-profile override + Stripe sessions; resolves Stripe app via billing profile.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Custom errorEncoder() must precede apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder(), not follow it.

key_file_guides.role: Sub-package gated by credits.enabled; ledger/accountResolver may be noops.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not assume injected ledger services are real implementations when credits are off.

key_file_guides.role: Sub-package listing flat-fee + usage-based charges; excludes meta.ChargeTypeCreditPurchase.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Credit-purchase charges belong to the credits API, never here.

anti_patterns: Extracting namespace from HTTP request params instead of h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
Hand-editing convert.gen.go instead of convert.go annotations + make generate
Omitting apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder() from AppendOptions — domain errors won't map to HTTP status
Mutating an existing customer without fetching it and checking IsDeleted()
Putting cross-package services (billing, credits, charges) on the parent handler struct instead of in their own sub-package

decisions.decision: One operation per file (create.go, delete.go, get.go, list.go, upsert.go)
decisions.rationale: Keeps each handler independently reviewable and bounds file growth as operations are added.
billing/credits/charges/entitlementaccess have distinct service deps and error encoders; co-locating in the parent would couple unrelated services.

api/v3/handlers/meters.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for all meter CRUD and query operations; bridges generated api/v3 request/response types to meter.ManageService and streaming.Connector via the httptransport.Handler pipeline, delegating all MeterQueryRequest-to-streaming.QueryParams translation to its query/ sub-package.

patterns.name: Type-alias triplet per operation file
patterns.description: create.go/get.go/update.go/query.go each declare <Op>Request/<Op>Response/<Op>Handler; path-param ops add <Op>Params.
patterns.example: type CreateMeterRequest = meter.CreateMeterInput; type CreateMeterResponse = api.Meter; type CreateMeterHandler httptransport.Handler[CreateMeterRequest, CreateMeterResponse]

patterns.name: NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param endpoints; NewHandler for no-param
patterns.description: meterID-scoped endpoints use NewHandlerWithArgs; CreateMeter uses NewHandler.
patterns.example: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoder, operation, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[Resp](http.StatusOK), opts...)

patterns.name: Namespace resolved in decoder, never in operation
patterns.description: h.resolveNamespace(ctx) is the first decoder call; errors return before the operation runs.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return GetMeterRequest{}, err }

patterns.name: Reserved-dimension validation in create/update decoders
patterns.description: validateDimensionsWithoutReserved(*body.Dimensions) (dimensions.go) delegates to query.IsReservedDimension and returns NewReservedDimensionError.
patterns.example: if body.Dimensions != nil { if err := validateDimensionsWithoutReserved(*body.Dimensions); err != nil { return CreateMeterRequest{}, err } }

patterns.name: Query endpoints delegate to query.BuildQueryParams
patterns.description: QueryMeter and QueryMeterCSV call query.BuildQueryParams(ctx, m, req.Body, query.NewCustomerResolver(h.customerService)); never inline param-building.
patterns.example: params, err := query.BuildQueryParams(ctx, m, req.Body, query.NewCustomerResolver(h.customerService))

patterns.name: ValidationIssue sentinel + WithPathString/WithAttr constructor
patterns.description: errors.go declares ErrReservedDimension with HTTP 400 attribute; per-call constructors add field-specific context.
patterns.example: func NewReservedDimensionError(d string) error { return ErrReservedDimension.WithPathString("dimensions", d).WithAttr("value", d) }

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (7 methods) and struct with resolveNamespace, service (meter.ManageService), streaming, customerService, options.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not put domain calls on the struct directly; they belong in per-operation files.

key_file_guides.role: Hand-written helpers (ToAPIMeterAggregation, ToAPIMeterQueryRow/Result, FromAPIUpdateMeterRequest) plus goverter annotations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Goverter vars (FromAPICreateMeterRequest, ToAPIMeter) are set in convert.gen.go init() — never call before init.

key_file_guides.role: Goverter output (DO NOT EDIT) initializing conversion vars in init().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Re-run make generate after changing convert.go annotations.

key_file_guides.role: ErrCodeReservedDimension code + ErrReservedDimension ValidationIssue sentinel.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New validation errors follow the sentinel + .WithPathString/.WithAttr constructor pattern.

key_file_guides.role: validateDimensionsWithoutReserved iterates a map against query.IsReservedDimension.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add new reserved dimensions in query/dimensions.go, not here.

key_file_guides.role: QueryMeterCSV + queryMeterCSVResult (commonhttp.CSVResponse); enriches rows with customer key/name via ListCustomers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new reserved column must be added to both the header slice and the record loop in Records().

key_file_guides.role: Sub-package owning BuildQueryParams, IsReservedDimension, CustomerResolverFunc, filter-operator extraction, ISO8601<->WindowSize.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never inline query translation in the parent files; always delegate to BuildQueryParams.

anti_patterns: Using NewHandler instead of NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param (meterID) endpoints
Calling h.resolveNamespace in the operation closure instead of the decoder
Adding a reserved dimension key in this package instead of query.IsReservedDimension
Hand-editing convert.gen.go (overwritten by go generate)
Omitting httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()), breaking error-to-status mapping

decisions.decision: query/ is a separate sub-package, not inline in query.go
decisions.rationale: Isolates dimension validation, filter extraction, customer resolution, and window-size mapping so handlers stay thin and the logic is testable without an HTTP context.

decisions.decision: Conversion functions are Goverter-generated vars set in init(), not methods
decisions.rationale: Lets type-safe generated mapping live in convert.gen.go while remaining callable from hand-written handlers without a receiver.

api/v3/handlers/plans.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for all plan CRUD and lifecycle operations (create, get, list, update, delete, publish, archive); bridges generated api/v3 types to plan.Service with rich bidirectional conversion of the Plan > Phase > RateCard > Price hierarchy. Its planaddons/ sub-package handles the plan-addons sub-resource.

patterns.name: Type-alias triplet per operation file
patterns.description: Each file declares <Op>Request/<Op>Response/<Op>Handler aliasing domain input/output directly.
patterns.example: type ArchivePlanRequest = plan.ArchivePlanInput; type ArchivePlanResponse = api.BillingPlan; type ArchivePlanHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[ArchivePlanRequest, ArchivePlanResponse, ArchivePlanParams]

patterns.name: Namespace resolved first in decoder
patterns.description: h.resolveNamespace(ctx) is the first decoder call; return immediately on error.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ArchivePlanRequest{}, err }

patterns.name: IgnoreNonCriticalIssues=true on create and update
patterns.description: After FromAPICreatePlanRequest/FromAPIUpsertPlanRequest, set req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true before returning from the decoder.
patterns.example: req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true; return req, nil

patterns.name: clock.Now() for time-sensitive fields
patterns.description: PublishPlan sets EffectiveFrom = clock.Now(); ArchivePlan sets EffectiveTo = clock.Now(). Never time.Now() — tests use clock.SetTime.
patterns.example: EffectiveTo: clock.Now()

patterns.name: Nil-check service response for mutating lifecycle ops
patterns.description: Create/Update/Publish/Archive check p == nil after the service call and return a descriptive error; Get/Delete do not.
patterns.example: if p == nil { return ArchivePlanResponse{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to archive plan") }; return ToAPIBillingPlan(*p)

patterns.name: Exhaustive price-type switch with unsupported guard
patterns.description: ToAPIBillingPrice switches on p.Type(); DynamicPriceType/PackagePriceType return models.NewGenericConflictError. ListPlans skips plans with unsupported prices via hasUnsupportedV3Price.
patterns.example: case productcatalog.DynamicPriceType: return result, models.NewGenericConflictError(fmt.Errorf("dynamic price is not supported in v3 API"))

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (7 methods) and struct holding only resolveNamespace, plan.Service, and options.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No streaming/customer service is injected; do not add deps — delegate to plan.Service.

key_file_guides.role: All bidirectional Plan>Phase>RateCard>Price conversion plus unsupportedV3PriceTypes map and hasUnsupportedV3Price.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Dynamic/Package prices return GenericConflictError (409), not 500; a new price type requires updating ToAPIBillingPrice, FromAPIBillingPrice, and unsupportedV3PriceTypes together.

key_file_guides.role: Table-driven tests for convert.go; uses clock.SetTime, newTestPlan, decimal/gobl literals.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New conversion paths need round-trip tests using typed API constants, not plain strings.

key_file_guides.role: ListPlans with page-based pagination; silently skips unsupported-v3-price plans (FIXME).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add further silent-skip logic — return errors or expose all items.

key_file_guides.role: PublishPlan sets EffectiveFrom = clock.Now().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must use clock.Now(), not time.Now().

key_file_guides.role: ArchivePlan sets EffectiveTo = clock.Now().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Same clock.Now() requirement.

key_file_guides.role: Sub-package for the plan-addons sub-resource bridging to planaddon.Service (injected separately from plan.Service).
key_file_guides.watch_for: planaddon.Service is a distinct injection from plan.Service.

anti_patterns: Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() in decoder closures
Omitting req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true in Create/Update decoders
Adding a price type in ToAPIBillingPrice without updating FromAPIBillingPrice, hasUnsupportedV3Price, and round-trip tests
Omitting apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder() from a handler options block
Calling domain service methods from the encoder (third) closure instead of the operation (second) closure

decisions.decision: Dynamic/Package price types return GenericConflictError (409) not 500
decisions.rationale: The v3 API has no wire format for these yet; a conflict error surfaces the limitation clearly instead of dropping data or returning a generic 500.

decisions.decision: IgnoreNonCriticalIssues set in the HTTP decoder, not the domain service
decisions.rationale: HTTP create/update callers expect lenient validation while the service still surfaces non-critical issues as warnings on the returned object.

api/v3/handlers/subscriptions.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for billing subscription lifecycle (list, get, create-from-plan, cancel, unschedule cancellation, change-plan); uses two distinct domain services — planSubscriptionService for create/change workflows and subscriptionService for get/list/cancel/continue. The subscriptionaddons/ sub-package lists subscription addons.

patterns.name: Two subscription services with split responsibilities
patterns.description: The handler struct holds planSubscriptionService (create/change, drives billing sync + plan versioning) and subscriptionService (get/list/cancel/continue). Using the wrong one silently changes workflow semantics.
patterns.example: h.planSubscriptionService.Create(ctx, request) // not h.subscriptionService.Create

patterns.name: Pre-validation of referenced entities in the decoder
patterns.description: Resolve and validate customer and plan refs inside the decoder via getCustomerByIDOrKey/getPlanByIDOrKey, returning apierrors.NewBadRequestError with InvalidParameters for missing required fields.
patterns.example: if body.Plan.Id == nil && body.Plan.Key == nil { return ..., apierrors.NewBadRequestError(ctx, errors.New(reason), []apierrors.InvalidParameter{{Field: "plan.id", Rule: "required", ...}}) }

patterns.name: PlanInput constructed via FromRef
patterns.description: After resolving a concrete plan, build plansubscription.PlanInput via planInput.FromRef(&PlanRefInput{Key, Version}) — never direct field assignment.
patterns.example: planInput := plansubscription.PlanInput{}; planInput.FromRef(&plansubscription.PlanRefInput{Key: planEntity.Key, Version: &planEntity.Version})

patterns.name: Timing union: AsDateTime() before enum
patterns.description: FromAPIBillingSubscriptionEditTiming tries AsDateTime() first, enum fallback second; order is load-bearing because RFC3339 strings also satisfy the enum string union.
patterns.example: if custom, err := t.AsDateTime(); err == nil { return subscription.Timing{Custom: &custom}, nil }

patterns.name: Cancel defaults timing to immediate
patterns.description: cancel.go sets timing.Enum = TimingImmediate when body.Timing is nil rather than requiring a timing value.
patterns.example: if body.Timing == nil { timing.Enum = lo.ToPtr(subscription.TimingImmediate) }

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (6 methods) and struct: resolveNamespace, customerService, planService, planSubscriptionService, subscriptionService.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mixing the two subscription services changes workflow semantics and billing sync.

key_file_guides.role: Creates subscription from plan; validates customer+plan, builds PlanInput via FromRef, calls planSubscriptionService.Create. Defines private getCustomerByIDOrKey/getPlanByIDOrKey helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Helpers are intentionally private to the handler (TODO to move to service layer).

key_file_guides.role: Change-to-plan; fetches current subscription for name/desc/metadata defaults, builds ChangeSubscriptionWorkflowInput, calls planSubscriptionService.Change.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Response is BillingSubscriptionChangeResponse{Current, Next}; metadata defaults from existing subscription unless body.Labels is non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: ToAPIBillingSubscription (clock.Now() for GetStatusAt), FromAPIBillingSubscriptionEditTiming (datetime-before-enum), FromAPIBillingSubscriptionCreate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Status tests must mock clock via clock.SetTime; reversing the timing decode order breaks custom RFC3339 timing.

key_file_guides.role: CancelSubscription; defaults nil timing to immediate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Nil timing must default to immediate, not error.

key_file_guides.role: Sub-package listing subscription addons; toAPISubscriptionAddon unions instance periods into one ActiveFrom/ActiveTo via clock.Now().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Sort validation uses subscriptionaddon.OrderBy.Validate() after field mapping.

anti_patterns: Swapping planSubscriptionService and subscriptionService — Create/Change must use the plan service for billing sync hooks
Reversing timing decode order (enum before datetime) — datetime strings get misidentified as enum
Calling planSubscriptionService without first resolving customer+plan via getByIDOrKey helpers
Using commonhttp.GenericErrorEncoder() instead of apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder() in v3 handlers
Setting PlanInput fields directly instead of planInput.FromRef

decisions.rationale: plansubscription.PlanSubscriptionService orchestrates billing sync and plan versioning atop core subscription.Service; the split keeps billing-coupling out of generic lifecycle and allows non-plan subscriptions later.

decisions.decision: datetime decoded before enum in timing decode
decisions.rationale: Both satisfy the string union; trying datetime first is the only way to correctly route RFC3339 custom timestamps without misidentifying them as enum values.

decisions.decision: Entity pre-validation happens in the decoder
decisions.rationale: Failing fast with apierrors.NewBadRequestError and typed InvalidParameters gives callers actionable field-level errors before any service call.
